headline: Iran Strikes Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan; Smoke Reported
summary: Iran launched missile strikes targeting the Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan, which hosts US military forces. Columns of smoke were reported rising from the facility following the attack. This strike broadens Iran's retaliatory campaign beyond its immediate neighbors to US-allied states hosting American military assets.

secondary_markets.name: Lockheed Martin
secondary_markets.reason: F-16 aircraft and THAAD/Patriot systems supplied to Jordan are now combat-relevant as Iranian strikes hit Jordanian airbases.

secondary_markets.name: Royal Jordanian Airlines
secondary_markets.reason: Iranian missile strike on Jordanian military airbase threatens civilian airspace safety and triggers route suspensions.

secondary_markets.name: RTX Corporation
secondary_markets.reason: Patriot air defense batteries in Jordan are now under active engagement, driving immediate interceptor replenishment orders.

secondary_markets.name: Gold
secondary_markets.reason: Expanding Iranian strikes to Jordan signals a multi-front war escalation, amplifying safe-haven demand for gold.

secondary_markets.name: Boeing
secondary_markets.reason: Boeing supplies F/A-18s and JDAM munitions to US forces in Jordan and benefits from emergency resupply contracts.

trade_position: Go long RTX as the geographic expansion of Iranian missile strikes to Jordan means US air defense missile inventories are being depleted across five simultaneous theaters (Israel, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Jordan), creating an unprecedented multi-front interceptor resupply emergency.
